Trump win fuels stock rally: Nasdaq up 400 points, gold plunges Yahoo Finance Anchor Rachelle Akuffo breaks down the top market stories of the day. All major indexes are in the green as investors react positively to the results of the US presidential election. The Nasdaq Composite (^IXIC) is up a notable 400 points today. The race for control of the House of Representatives remains in focus. The Democrats have won 181 seats while the Republicans have 199 seats, with 55 seats yet to be called. However, the Republicans have secured a majority in the Senate. Meanwhile, gold (GC=F) prices are falling as investors shift away from the traditional safe-haven asset and embrace the Trump trade instead. Stocks in the green energy sector that were previously buoyed by the prospect of a Democratic victory are now plummeting in the wake of Trumps win. Another stock in the spotlight is Meta Platforms (META) as the Supreme Court is set to hear an appeal from the tech giant regarding a securities fraud lawsuit today. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Wealth here. This post was written by Angel Smith

Why GE Vernova is this analysts energy sector pick under Trump President-elect Donald Trump has picked Liberty Energy (LBRT) CEO Chris Wright as his pick for secretary of the Department of Energy (DOE). Wright also sits on the board of nuclear energy provider Oklo Inc. (OKLO). William Blair group head of energy and sustainability research sector Jed Dorsheimer joins Julie Hyman and Madison Mills on Market Domination Overtime to discuss what stocks within the energy sector benefit the most from a second Trump administration. Wright is a fantastic pick [for DOE secretary] in terms of GE Vernova (GEV), Dorsheimer says, adding that the company is the worlds leader in energy in terms of its market share making natural gas turbines and switchgear substations. Watch the video above for Dorsheimers perspective on Trump-era energy policies, the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) data centers, how Trumps proposed Department of Government Efficiency Commission (DOGE), co-led by Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy could affect the future of nuclear energy, and more. These sectors will outperform in 2025: Portfolio mgr. F/M Investments senior vice president and senior portfolio manager Francisco Bido offers a preview of the market landscape headed into 2025, highlighting key sectors and transformative trends on Wealth! Bido identifies information technology and retail as his top two sectors for the upcoming year. Within these sectors, he spotlights companies like AMD (AMD), First Solar (FSLR), Palantir (PLTR), and Burlington Coat Factory (BURL) as promising investment opportunities. Artificial intelligence emerges as a standout sector with significant growth potential, especially as data centers become increasingly prevalent. This is just the beginning of a long-term ramp-up, so its gonna carry into 2025, 2026, and God knows when, Bido explains, emphasizing the sustained momentum of AI innovation.
